prompt_text:
I'm going to describe two scenarios. In the memory scenario, a novel room called a ulmp has a property: it can be activated by one of its colored fixtures. Your job is to figure out which room in the perception scenario is the ulmp-analog, and therefore which colored fixture on it can be activated.

Memory scenario: There are three rooms in a building: a ulmp, a rask, and a dask. The ulmp is-directly-below the rask. The rask is-adjacent-to the dask. The ulmp has a blue colored fixture on top, a green colored fixture on side, and a red colored fixture on bottom. The rask has a green colored fixture on top and a blue colored fixture on bottom. The dask has a red colored fixture on top and a blue colored fixture on side. Operating the blue fixture in the ulmp activates it.

Perception scenario: There are three rooms in a building: a zop, a drog, and a glim. The glim is-directly-below the drog. The drog is-adjacent-to the zop. The zop has a red colored fixture on top and a green colored fixture on bottom. The drog has a green colored fixture on top and a blue colored fixture on side. The glim has a blue colored fixture on top, a green colored fixture on side, and a red colored fixture on bottom.

Which room in the perception scenario is the ulmp-analog, and which colored fixture activates it?
